title: Amy Madigan's Oscar Win: Why It Inspires
text: Amy Madigan won Best Supporting Actress at the 98th Academy Awards for her role as Aunt Gladys in the horror film Weapons—a surprising victory for a genre film and its 75-year-old star. Madigan's win was remarkable because horror performances rarely win major awards. Director Zach Cregger's film received minimal Oscar recognition otherwise, making her the sole representative. Her character alternated between grandiosity and quiet menace as a supernatural villain, delivering an all-out performance. Madigan had experienced years of professional rejection, becoming accustomed to the phone not ringing. Her previous Oscar nomination came in 1985 for Twice in a Lifetime. Yet she brought unexpected depth to the role, imbuing it with richness and life that transcended the script's limitations. This victory should inspire performers discovering their opportunities aren't expired, and imaginative directors recognizing the value in rediscovering overlooked talent.
